description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who has just received log files from three worker nodes that ran a distributed training job.  
Your goal is to prepare two small artefacts that the research team will use for quick debugging.

Existing files & layout  
  • All raw logs are already present in /home/user/logs/  
       ├── node1.log  
       ├── node2.log  
       └── node3.log  

Required output files  
Create a new directory /home/user/data/debug (make it if it does not yet exist) and inside it generate two files with the exact names, relative ordering and byte-for-byte contents described below.

1. /home/user/data/debug/error_summary.csv  
   • This must be a CSV with exactly 1 header row followed by one data row for each node (total: 4 rows).  
   • Header must be precisely:  
         node,error_count  
     (all lowercase, no extra spaces).  
   • Each subsequent row must contain:  
         node name , comma , number of lines in that node’s log that contain the substring “ERROR”.  
   • Sort the rows lexicographically by node name (node1, node2, node3).  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline (UNIX line ending).

   Example of the exact required layout (values will be checked by the grader):  
         node,error_count  
         node1,2  
         node2,1  
         node3,2  

2. /home/user/data/debug/gradient_issues.log  
   • This plain-text log must contain every line (verbatim) from any node’s log that includes the case-sensitive phrase “Gradient explosion”.  
   • Preserve the original timestamped line exactly as it appears in the source log (no trimming, no extra characters).  
   • Lines must be ordered by timestamp as they appeared across the whole cluster (oldest first).  
   • Write one line per entry and terminate the file with a single trailing newline (UNIX line ending).

How to get there  
Use whatever standard shell utilities you prefer (e.g., mkdir, grep, awk, wc, sort, cut, etc.).  
The entire task can be finished with just 2–3 well-chosen commands.

Once both files are created exactly as specified, you are done.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts)
---------------------------------------
Directory /home/user/logs/ already exists and contains three files:

1. /home/user/logs/node1.log
   [2024-04-24 12:00:00] INFO  Starting training epoch 1
   [2024-04-24 12:00:01] ERROR Loss became NaN
   [2024-04-24 12:00:02] ERROR Gradient explosion detected
   [2024-04-24 12:00:03] INFO  Retrying with smaller learning rate

2. /home/user/logs/node2.log
   [2024-04-24 12:00:00] INFO  Starting training epoch 1
   [2024-04-24 12:00:05] ERROR Out of memory
   [2024-04-24 12:00:06] INFO  Swapped to CPU memory

3. /home/user/logs/node3.log
   [2024-04-24 12:00:00] INFO  Starting training epoch 1
   [2024-04-24 12:00:04] ERROR Gradient explosion detected
   [2024-04-24 12:00:05] ERROR Gradient explosion detected
   [2024-04-24 12:00:06] INFO  Adjusting gradient clipping

Target state after successful task completion
---------------------------------------------
Directory /home/user/data/debug/ exists and contains two files with the exact following contents (including newlines, case, spacing):

1. /home/user/data/debug/error_summary.csv  
   node,error_count\n
   node1,2\n
   node2,1\n
   node3,2\n

2. /home/user/data/debug/gradient_issues.log  
   [2024-04-24 12:00:02] ERROR Gradient explosion detected\n
   [2024-04-24 12:00:04] ERROR Gradient explosion detected\n
   [2024-04-24 12:00:05] ERROR Gradient explosion detected\n
